title: Module 1
content:
Number Systems: Binary, Octal, and Hexadecimal - Representation of
negative numbers in binary -binary arithmetic
Binary Codes: BCD, BCD addition, Excess-3, Gray code. Concept of
parity Logic Gates: Introduction to logic families. Basic gates and universal
gates, Realisation of logic gates using transistors and diodes, internal
diagram and basic working of TTL NAND and CMOS NOT gates.

title: Module 2
content:
Minimization Techniques
Boolean Algebra - Operations, Laws and theorems, De Morgan’s theorems,
SOP and POS Boolean expressions, truth tables, Karnaugh map (up to 4
variables), Minterm and Maxterm expansions, incompletely specified
functions, formation of switching functions from word statement,
realization using basic gates and universal gates
Functions of combinational logic
Adders and subtractors, ripple carry adders, BCD adder, binary to gray and
gray to binary code converters, comparators, multiplexers, demultiplexers,
decoders, encoders.

title: Module 3
content:
Sequential Logic circuits
Latches – SR Latch. Flip- Flops – SR, JK, D & T Flip Flops – Level &
Edge triggered flip flops – Synchronous & Asynchronous inputs -
Conversion between flip flops. Master slave flip flops, waveform diagrams
Shift Registers
SISO, SIPO, PISO, PIPO shift registers, Right & Left shifts, Universal shift
registers

title: Module 4
content:
Counters
Asynchronous counters- Up, Down and Up/ Down counter, Mod n counters
– Ring counter- Johnson counter. Introduction to design of synchronous
sequential circuits using Finite State Machines - Mealy & Moore types with
single input-single out problems-design of sequence detectors, synchronous
counters and sequence generators
